title: Migrate .github/skills into .cursor/skills for Cursor IDE continuity
description:
Context: Cursor often injects only skills under .cursor/skills/, while Conscius also maintains .github/skills/ (e.g. planning, mulch, guardrails, session, pr-review, docs, git-workflow) — agents miss them when not listed.

Scope: Audit .github/skills vs .cursor/skills; for each skill missing or stale in .cursor, copy or reconcile content into .cursor/skills/ (same folder names / SKILL.md pattern). Decide single source of truth: either (A) .cursor canonical for Cursor and keep .github as mirror for GitHub Copilot, or (B) one canonical tree with the other symlinked/README pointer — document in CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md.

Acceptance: All project skills agents need in Cursor exist under .cursor/skills/ with up-to-date content; duplication strategy documented; no broken references.
close_reason: Synced .github/skills into .cursor/skills (planning+sections, pr-review, guardrails, docs, git-workflow, mulch, session); documented canonical .github source and Copilot markers in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md.

title: Epic 1 — Monorepo Foundation & Shared Types
Epic 3 — @conscius/agent-plugin-beads
Epic 2 — @conscius/agent-core
Interactive Mulch record helper (script + Make target)
description:
After P0 skills migration (coreai-na7), reduce friction for persisting Mulch expertise.

Deliverables:
- scripts/mulch-record-interactive.sh (or .bash): prompts for record type (convention|pattern|failure|decision|reference|guide), asks only required fields per ml record --help, defaults domain from .mulch/mulch.config.yaml when a single domain is listed, optional tags/files/evidence-bead; runs ml record with quoted args.
- Root Makefile with target mulch-record (and help line) invoking the script; document in .mulch/README.md.

Acceptance: engineer can run make mulch-record from repo root without memorizing ml record flags; script exits non-zero on cancel/validation failure; documented in .mulch/README.md.
acceptance_criteria: make mulch-record runs interactive script; README updated; script defaults domain from mulch.config.yaml
close_reason: Added scripts/mulch-record-interactive.sh, root Makefile mulch-record + help, .mulch/README.md; mulch skills table row.

title: MVP-4: runtime.run() → prompt
description: Public API: runtime.run(input: string): string — one full cycle, return final assembled prompt as a single string ONLY. Must NOT return MemorySegment[], summaries object, or other structured types. Align with conscius run. Tests.

title: MVP-2: Runtime guardrails (basic)
description: In @conscius/runtime: guardrails = simple string heuristics only (patterns, denylists, basic checks). Filter/drop risky segments before buildPromptContext. NO external services, NO policy engine, NO ML classifiers. Tests.

title: MVP-5: Plugin contract — memory-only prompt influence
description: Enforce that prompt output derives only from memorySegments (not from ad-hoc host fields). Document + tests; tighten types/runtime if needed.

title: MVP-3: CLI full cycle
description: Implement conscius run --input "...": load config+plugins, full runtime cycle, print final prompt string to stdout. Canonical CLI binary name is conscius (@conscius/cli). Tests.

title: MVP-1: Runtime compression (basic)
description: In @conscius/runtime: MVP compression must use NO LLM calls and NO summarisation — ONLY deduplication (existing), priority-based segment trimming, and segment count / approximate token limit before prompt build. Tests.

title: Epic 11 — Runtime MVP Hardening
description:
Complete v3 MVP per Definition of Done below. Spec: docs/planning/beads-mvp-alignment-v3.md, docs/specs/runtime-v3.md

## Definition of Done (MVP)

MVP is complete when:

- `runtime.run(input: string): string` — returns the final assembled prompt text only (not segments, not a structured object).
- Segment ordering for prompt build is deterministic.
- Compression (MVP-1) reduces segment payload deterministically using ONLY deduplication, priority-based trimming, and count/token limits — NO LLM calls, NO summarisation.
- Guardrails (MVP-2) filter unsafe segments using simple string heuristics only — NO external services, NO policy engine.
- CLI: `conscius run` with `--input` executes the full runtime cycle and prints the final prompt to stdout.
- Plugins affect the assembled prompt only via `memorySegments`; non-memory host fields do not change prompt output.

No additional features beyond these for Epic 11.

title: Refactor mulchAdapter — replace mulch search with ml prime
description:
## Problem

`mulchAdapter.ts` (~250 lines) calls `mulch search <topic>` with custom JSONL parsing, PATH resolution, fallback file reading, and deduplication. Neither `ml` nor `mulch` is installed locally so the read path is entirely non-functional. `onSessionEnd` iterates `pendingMulchLessons` but nothing ever populates that array — also non-functional.

## Key finding from source audit

`ml prime` takes **domain names** not topic strings: `ml prime [domains...]`. The original plan used `ml prime <topic>` which was wrong. Corrected approach: call `ml prime` with no args (all domains, budget-limited ~4000 tokens).

Also: `@os-eco/mulch-cli` requires **Bun** as runtime (`#!/usr/bin/env bun`). Two prerequisites: bun + @os-eco/mulch-cli.

## Decision: drop staging for MVP

`candidates.jsonl` / `pendingMulchLessons` / `onSessionEnd` dropped from plugin hooks. Interactive agents call `ml record` directly. Plugin is read-only at lifecycle level.

## Changes

### mulchAdapter.ts — full replacement
Remove: all old logic (~200 lines).
Add: resolveMlExecutable, assertMlRunnable (verifies bun), runMlInit, runMlPrime (no topic arg).
API change: queryMulch(topic, repoRoot) → queryMulch(repoRoot). Return type: MulchLesson[] → string.

### hooks.ts — setup guard; remove onSessionEnd
Add ensureMlReady(repoRoot): 3-step guard (ml on PATH? → bun present? → .mulch/ initialised?).
Remove getTopicHint, formatLesson. Simplify onSessionStart to: ensureMlReady → queryMulch → push stdout.
Remove onSessionEnd entirely.

### Tests — full rewrite
mulchAdapter.spec.ts: ml prime called, throws on missing ml, throws on missing bun, ml init runs.
hooks.spec.ts: setup guard tested, onSessionEnd tests removed.
lessonWriter.spec.ts: unchanged.

### README
Prerequisites (bun + @os-eco/mulch-cli), automated vs manual, error reference with exact messages.

### Error messages
- ml not found → install @os-eco/mulch-cli
- bun not found → install bun (curl instructions)
- ml init failed → show stderr
- ml prime failed → show stderr

## Acceptance criteria
- ml prime (no topic arg) called via execFile; stdout pushed to promptSegments
- ml not found → throws with install instructions
- bun not found → throws with bun install instructions
- .mulch/ absent → ml init runs automatically
- onSessionEnd removed from plugin
- lessonWriter.ts still exports writeMulchLesson; lessonWriter.spec.ts unchanged
- README covers bun + ml prerequisites, automation, manual steps, all error scenarios
- npx nx run-many -t typecheck,lint,test --projects=agent-plugin-mulch,agent-types passes
design:
## Implementation Plan

Full plan: `.copilot/session-state/849bc280-31f9-4b7c-921a-b6b92a075659/plan.md`

### Prerequisites (verified against @os-eco/mulch-cli v0.6.3 source)

- `@os-eco/mulch-cli` requires **Bun** runtime (`#!/usr/bin/env bun`, raw TypeScript source)
- Install chain: `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash` then `npm install -g @os-eco/mulch-cli`
- `ml prime [domains...]` takes domain names, NOT topic strings. With no args: all domains, budget-limited (~4000 tokens)
- `ml prime` output: pre-formatted markdown with session-end reminder

### Architecture: mulchAdapter.ts (full replacement)

Remove (~200 lines): resolveMulchExecutable, runMulchSearch, parseMulchOutput, parseLesson, isMulchLesson, isStringArray, matchesTopic, readMulchFile, deduplication.

Add (~40 lines):
- `resolveMlExecutable()` — resolve `ml` on PATH; throw with `npm install -g @os-eco/mulch-cli` if missing
- `assertMlRunnable()` — exec `ml --version` to verify bun is present; throw with `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash` if missing
- `runMlInit(repoRoot)` — `execFile(ml, ['init'], {cwd: repoRoot})`
- `runMlPrime(repoRoot)` — `execFile(ml, ['prime'], {cwd: repoRoot})` → stdout string
- `queryMulch(repoRoot)` — resolve → assert → prime → return stdout

Breaking API change: `queryMulch(topic, repoRoot): Promise<MulchLesson[]>` → `queryMulch(repoRoot): Promise<string>`

### Architecture: hooks.ts (setup guard + simplify)

Add `ensureMlReady(repoRoot)`: 3-step guard:
1. Is `ml` on PATH? → throw with install instructions
2. Can `ml` execute (is bun present)? → throw with bun install instructions
3. Is `.mulch/` initialised? → auto-run `ml init`

Simplify `onSessionStart`: ensureMlReady → queryMulch → push stdout to promptSegments.
Remove `onSessionEnd` entirely. Remove `getTopicHint`, `formatLesson`.

### README (full rewrite)

Sections: What it does | Prerequisites (bun + ml) | First-time setup (automatic) | Recording lessons (engineer's responsibility, 3 examples) | Programmatic usage | Errors and troubleshooting (4 scenarios) | What is NOT automated

### Error messages (exact wording)

1. ml not found: `agent-plugin-mulch: ml is required but was not found on PATH.\nInstall it with: npm install -g @os-eco/mulch-cli`
2. bun not found: `agent-plugin-mulch: ml requires Bun to run, but Bun was not found.\nInstall Bun: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash\nThen restart your terminal.`
3. ml init failed: `agent-plugin-mulch: ml init failed in {repoRoot}\n{stderr}`
4. ml prime failed: `agent-plugin-mulch: ml prime failed\n{stderr}`
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional
- [ ] `ml prime` (no topic arg) called via `execFile`; stdout pushed to `promptSegments`
- [ ] `ml` not found → throws with exact install instructions
- [ ] bun not found → throws with exact bun install instructions
- [ ] `.mulch/` absent → `ml init` runs automatically before query
- [ ] `onSessionEnd` removed from plugin — no write path in hooks
- [ ] `lessonWriter.ts` still exports `writeMulchLesson`; `lessonWriter.spec.ts` unchanged
- [ ] README covers: bun + ml prerequisites, automation, manual steps, all 4 error scenarios

### Quality gates
- [ ] `npx nx run-many -t typecheck,lint,test --projects=agent-plugin-mulch,agent-types` passes

## Testing Strategy

### Automated tests (Jest, mocked — run in CI, no bun/ml needed)

**mulchAdapter.spec.ts** (10 tests):
1. resolveMlExecutable finds ml on PATH
2. resolveMlExecutable throws when ml not on PATH (with install instructions)
3. assertMlRunnable succeeds when bun is present
4. assertMlRunnable throws when bun is missing (with bun install instructions)
5. runMlInit calls ml init with correct cwd
6. runMlInit throws on failure (includes stderr)
7. runMlPrime calls ml prime with no args
8. runMlPrime returns stdout as-is
9. runMlPrime throws on failure (includes stderr)
10. queryMulch returns ml prime stdout directly

**hooks.spec.ts** (9 tests):
1. onSessionStart calls ensureMlReady then queryMulch
2. onSessionStart pushes ml prime stdout to promptSegments
3. onSessionStart skips when ml prime returns empty string
4. onSessionStart throws when ml is not installed
5. onSessionStart throws when bun is missing
6. onSessionStart calls ml init when .mulch/ absent
7. onSessionStart skips ml init when .mulch/ exists
8. Plugin has name property 'agent-plugin-mulch'
9. Plugin has no onSessionEnd method

**lessonWriter.spec.ts** (5 tests, unchanged):
1. Creates dir and appends JSONL lesson
2. Appends without overwriting
3. Fails on missing required fields
4. Fails on invalid ISO 8601
5. Fails on invalid type

### Manual testing checklist (engineer, pre-merge, one-time)

- [ ] M1: Remove ml from PATH → error message shows install command
- [ ] M2: Have ml on PATH, remove bun → error message shows bun install command
- [ ] M3: Delete .mulch/ dir → auto-created by ml init
- [ ] M4: Add expertise records → queryMulch returns formatted markdown with domains
- [ ] M5: Empty .mulch/expertise/ → queryMulch returns empty string
- [ ] M6: Full plugin lifecycle via mulchPlugin.onSessionStart(context) → promptSegments populated
notes:
**Architectural review completed 2026-03-19**

Source audit of @os-eco/mulch-cli v0.6.3 confirmed all plan assumptions. 6 findings:

1. ✅ All 16 technical assumptions verified against upstream source (prime.ts, init.ts, cli.ts, record.ts, config.ts, format.ts)
2. ⚠️ Empty-domain edge case: ml prime outputs guidance text even with no domains — ACCEPTED (upstream design intent, useful for agent awareness)
3. ⚠️ Version requirement changed: ≥ 0.6.0 → ≥ 0.6.3 (only verified version)
4. ⚠️ Added 10s timeout to all execFile calls (prevents indefinite hangs)
5. ⚠️ Use access() not deprecated fs.exists() for .mulch/ check
6. ℹ️ README: added domain explanation, fresh-repo guidance message note

Test count increased from 24 → 26 (added timeout tests).

Plan file: .copilot/session-state/849bc280-31f9-4b7c-921a-b6b92a075659/plan.md
close_reason: Refactor complete on main: ml prime path, Bun guard, no onSessionEnd in plugin; mulchAdapter.ts uses execFile ml prime/init.

title: Mulch candidate review — surface, approve, and promote lessons at session end
description:
## Problem

`writeMulchLesson` stages lessons to `.mulch/candidates.jsonl` but there is no mechanism that surfaces them for review. Lessons silently accumulate and are never promoted to live memory unless the user manually opens the file. This makes the existing mulch write pipeline functionally incomplete.

## Goal

Close the loop: after lessons are staged, the agent surfaces them, the user approves or rejects each one, and approved lessons are promoted to the canonical store.

---

## Scope

### 1. Surface candidates at session end (`hooks.ts`)

After writing new lessons in `onSessionEnd`, read back what was just staged and present each lesson to the user:

```
## Mulch Candidates — Review Required

1. [lesson-storage-triage-001] Use the right storage layer for the right type of information
   Recommendation: For planning flags and sequencing decisions...
   Classification: foundational | Type: decision | Tags: memory, storage

Approve all? (yes / no / review each)
```

Present via `context.promptSegments` or a dedicated `context.pendingReview` output channel (whichever the core runtime supports).

### 2. Add `promoteMulchLesson()` to lessonWriter.ts

CLI-first, file-fallback pattern (same as `queryMulch`):

- If `ml` CLI is available: call `ml promote <id>`
- Fallback: append the lesson JSON to `.mulch/expertise/<topic-slug>.jsonl`, creating the file if needed
- Remove promoted entry from `candidates.jsonl`

```ts
export async function promoteMulchLesson(
  lessonId: string,
  repoRoot: string
): Promise<void>
```

### 3. Add `rejectMulchLesson()` to lessonWriter.ts

Remove the entry from `candidates.jsonl` by id — no promotion.

```ts
export async function rejectMulchLesson(
  lessonId: string,
  repoRoot: string
): Promise<void>
```

### 4. Wire review into onSessionEnd

```ts
async onSessionEnd(context: AgentContext): Promise<void> {
  // ... existing write loop ...

  // Surface staged lessons for review
  const staged = await loadStagedCandidates(repoRoot);
  if (staged.length > 0) {
    context.pendingReview = staged; // or push to promptSegments
  }
}
```

---

## What is NOT changed

- `candidates.jsonl` format unchanged
- Human always has final say — no auto-promotion
- Existing `writeMulchLesson` behaviour unchanged

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `onSessionEnd` surfaces newly staged lessons at the end of each session
- [ ] `promoteMulchLesson(id)` moves lesson to `.mulch/expertise/<slug>.jsonl` (or via `ml` if available) and removes from candidates
- [ ] `rejectMulchLesson(id)` removes lesson from candidates without promoting
- [ ] CLI-first / file-fallback pattern used for promotion (consistent with `queryMulch`)
- [ ] Unit tests cover: promote (with and without ml CLI), reject, surface with no candidates, surface with multiple candidates
- [ ] No lessons are silently lost between sessions
close_reason: Superseded by coreai-f7m. MVP decision: drop staging pipeline entirely — no onSessionEnd, no pendingMulchLessons, no promote/reject flow. Engineers call `ml record` directly. If programmatic staging is needed later, create a new task scoped to that requirement.

title: Mulch lesson taxonomy: add MulchLessonTag union type, make tags required
description:
Define a stable 7-value tag taxonomy for MulchLesson to enable category-aware retrieval and consistent human review.

Tags: convention | bug-pattern | architecture | tooling | workflow | testing | context

Changes:
1. agent-types: add MulchLessonTag union type; change tags from string[]? to MulchLessonTag[] (required, min 1)
2. lessonWriter.ts: validate tags is present and contains at least one valid MulchLessonTag
3. All test fixtures (lessonWriter.spec.ts, hooks.spec.ts, mulchAdapter.spec.ts, hook-runner.spec.ts): add valid tags
4. docs/api/agent-types.md + skills/mulch/SKILL.md + copilot-instructions.md: document taxonomy
close_reason: Taxonomy aligned with upstream ml schema: MULCH_LESSON_TYPES, MULCH_LESSON_CLASSIFICATIONS, free-form tags. Tests pass, pushed to main.

title: Mulch lesson staging: write candidates.jsonl instead of direct mulch.jsonl write
description:
At onSessionEnd, instead of writing directly to .mulch/mulch.jsonl, write pending lessons to .mulch/candidates.jsonl (staging file). This adds a human-review gate before lessons reach the live memory store.

Changes required:
1. lessonWriter.ts — change write target from .mulch/mulch.jsonl to .mulch/candidates.jsonl; rename constants accordingly
2. hook-runner write permissions — add .mulch/candidates.jsonl to default approved writes
3. Update lessonWriter.spec.ts and hooks.spec.ts to reflect new path
4. Update .github/skills/mulch/SKILL.md to document the candidates review step
5. Update agent-core README and Epic 4 spec doc if they mention the direct write path
close_reason: Staged to candidates.jsonl — human review gate in place before lessons reach live mulch.jsonl store

title: Epic 4 — @conscius/agent-plugin-mulch
description:
Surface relevant Mulch experience lessons at onSessionStart and persist explicit pending lessons at onSessionEnd without forking Mulch. Canonical direction for this repo is upstream `ml` for docs and new integration work; keep any legacy `mulch` compatibility lightweight.

Final state: mulchAdapter, onSessionStart hooks, explicit lessonWriter helper, upstream-ml-aligned write-permission changes, pendingMulchLessons session-end wiring, and unit tests are implemented and validated with Nx checks.
close_reason: Epic 4 implementation completed: adapter, session-start injection, explicit lesson writing, session-end wiring, permission alignment, and tests all implemented and validated.

title: Pin workspace inter-package deps before publish

title: E4 follow-up: wire explicit Mulch lessons into onSessionEnd
description: Design and implement the explicit lesson source/API for session-end persistence. This follow-up should decide how lessons are supplied to the Mulch plugin at session end without inventing heuristic extraction from conversation history. Once that source exists, wire `onSessionEnd` to persist those supplied lessons.
close_reason: Added pendingMulchLessons to AgentContext and wired agent-plugin-mulch onSessionEnd to persist explicit lessons with tests.

title: E4 follow-up: align Mulch storage and write-path assumptions with upstream ml
description: Review the remaining Epic 4 writer work against upstream `ml` storage/layout expectations and this repo's current approved write-path assumptions. Decide the smallest viable change needed so lesson persistence can follow the canonical `ml` direction without over-architecting legacy compatibility.
close_reason: Aligned agent-core write-permission defaults and docs for upstream ml expertise storage while retaining lightweight legacy .mulch/mulch.jsonl compatibility; validated with Nx checks.

tsconfig.spec.json must have customConditions: null (avoids TS5098). See agent-plugin-beads/tsconfig.spec.json.
notes: Added lessonWriter unit tests for explicit JSONL persistence and validation failures. Remaining test work depends on the follow-up task that wires explicit lessons into onSessionEnd.
close_reason: Mulch adapter, hook, lessonWriter, and onSessionEnd flows are covered by unit tests and validated with Nx checks.

title: E3-T3: contextLoader.ts — loads spec file from task metadata
E3-T4: Unit tests with mocked bd CLI (27 tests)
E3-T1: beadsAdapter.ts — calls bd show, parses BeadsTask
E3-T2: hooks.ts — onTaskStart injects task metadata
E2-T4: CLI — agent start/end/task commands
E2-T5: Unit tests (57 tests)
E2-T1: Context builder — assembles prompt, triggers compression
E2-T2: Plugin loader — loads plugins, calls lifecycle hooks
E2-T3: Hook runner — resolves hooks, enforces write permissions
E1-T2: Configure Nx targets and Prettier
E1-T1: Scaffold @conscius/agent-types
Epic 7 — @conscius/agent-plugin-guardrails
description: Validation pipeline triggered when a Beads task enters 'review' status. Runs: format → lint → typecheck → unit tests → integration tests. Pass → close task. Fail → reopen to in_progress.
close_reason: pre-v3 epic frozen — do not execute as written; replace with Runtime MVP Hardening + future v3 epics

title: Epic 6 — @conscius/agent-plugin-compression
description: Ephemeral conversation compression — NEVER writes to disk. Groups messages into topic segments, compresses older ones into CompressionSummary (100-200 words each) at threshold.
close_reason: pre-v3 epic frozen — do not execute as written; replace with Runtime MVP Hardening + future v3 epics

title: Epic 5 — @conscius/agent-plugin-session
description: Manages SESSION.md lifecycle — reads at session start, writes structured update at session end. Max 500 words. Automates what is currently done manually.

title: Epic 9 — @conscius/skillshare
notes: ⚠️ DO NOT START this epic without first discussing the plan with the user. The skillshare scope needs to be re-evaluated against current needs before implementation begins. Agreed 2026-03-18.

description: Convenience bundle — installs all 5 plugins + agent-core as peer deps. Provides 'agent-stack-standard init' to scaffold a default config.

notes: Post-MVP (per beads-mvp-alignment): mulch memory qualification / ranking / filtering excluded from MVP. Mulch MVP = experience segments only.
